checklist: Confirm WebBLE is installed from the App Store on this iPhone.
Settings → Apps → Safari → Extensions → WebBLE: Allow Extension is ON and Permission → Other Websites is Allow.
Grant 'Always Allow on Every Website' for WebBLE via the aA button on the address bar → Manage Extensions → WebBLE.
Verify the tab is not in Private Browsing (extensions may be inert).
Page is served over HTTPS (Web Bluetooth requires a secure context).
Reload the page after toggling permissions so the extension re-injects.
Check navigator.bluetooth.getAvailability() — if it returns false, the extension is installed but not reachable on this origin.
extension-not-detected.common_fix: Open Settings → Apps → Safari → Extensions → WebBLE and toggle Allow Extension ON, then grant 'Always Allow on Every Website'. Reload the page. navigator.bluetooth.getAvailability() should then return true.

checklist: Is Safari backgrounded? iOS suspends the extension's BLE session when the tab loses focus — foreground only, or register a keep-alive via window.webbleIOS.backgroundSync.requestBackgroundConnection.
Is the peripheral in range and powered? BLE drops around 10 m / 30 ft unobstructed and when the peripheral battery dies.
Are you re-using a BluetoothRemoteGATTService / Characteristic handle from before a disconnect? Handles are invalidated — re-fetch after reconnect.
Are you calling gatt.connect() concurrently from multiple call sites? Serialize reconnects.
Is 'gattserverdisconnected' handled with exponential-backoff reconnect logic?
Is another app on the phone holding the peripheral? iOS multiplexes CoreBluetooth but some peripherals only accept one central.
device-disconnects.common_fix: Add a gattserverdisconnected listener and reconnect on user interaction. For background-safe operation, register window.webbleIOS.backgroundSync.requestBackgroundConnection({ deviceId: device.id }) to have the companion app hold the link while Safari is backgrounded.

checklist: Is the service listed in filters or optionalServices of the requestDevice() call? Access is gated by that list — SecurityError otherwise.
Does the peripheral actually expose the characteristic? Call service.getCharacteristics() and log the UUIDs the peripheral returns.
Does the characteristic support the operation you issued? Check char.properties { read, write, writeWithoutResponse, notify, indicate }.
Is the handle fresh? A handle captured before a disconnect fails with NetworkError after reconnect — re-fetch service/characteristic after every gatt.connect().
Did you await gatt.connect() before getPrimaryService()?
Is the peripheral bonded where bonding is required? Some characteristics reject reads/writes until pairing completes.
gatt-operation-failed.common_fix: Declare every service you plan to touch in filters/optionalServices at requestDevice() time, re-fetch service and characteristic handles after every reconnect, and consult char.properties before calling read/write/startNotifications.

checklist: Notification permission granted? Call window.webbleIOS.backgroundSync.requestPermission() and expect 'granted' — 'denied' means the user must flip Settings → Notifications → WebBLE manually.
Has the WebBLE companion app been launched at least once? Background sync runs inside the app; registrations persist but nothing consumes them until the app has registered its background modes.
Is cooldownSeconds throttling your events? Minimum 5 s is enforced — set it low only if your product truly needs that rate.
Is template.url same-origin with the registering page? Cross-origin urls are dropped silently at fire time.
Does the condition ever match? operator:'gt', threshold:150 only fires when the decoded value exceeds 150 — use 'always' or 'changed' to confirm the pipeline end-to-end.
Is the peripheral actually notifying in the background? Some peripherals drop subscriptions under iOS background constraints.
notifications-not-firing.common_fix: Call requestPermission() before registering, launch the WebBLE companion app at least once, then test with a 'changed' or 'always' operator to confirm the pipeline end-to-end before narrowing the condition.
